title: Hotel Search Performance Improvement

summary.overview: This PR optimizes the hotel search pipeline by introducing Elasticsearch query batching, response caching with Redis, and a streamlined serialization path. Load testing shows a 65% reduction in average response time (from 850ms to 290ms) and a 40% reduction in database load during peak search traffic. The implementation is clean, well-tested, and follows established performance optimization patterns.

riskAssessment.summary: The changes are isolated to the search read path and do not affect booking or payment flows. The caching layer includes proper invalidation logic and TTL configuration. Performance improvements have been validated through load testing with production-representative data volumes.
items: Cache invalidation relies on hotel inventory update events; if events are delayed, stale search results may be served for a brief window
Elasticsearch multi-search batch size should be monitored in production to ensure it stays within cluster resource limits

strengths: Excellent use of Elasticsearch multi-search API to batch multiple filter queries into a single round-trip, reducing network overhead by 70%
Well-implemented cache-aside pattern with configurable TTL and proper cache key generation that accounts for all search parameters including geo-bounds
Thorough performance test suite with benchmarks that validate latency targets at P50, P95, and P99 percentiles
Clean refactoring of the serialization path using System.Text.Json source generators for zero-allocation response serialization
Good observability with custom metrics for cache hit ratio, Elasticsearch query latency, and batch size distribution

recommendations: Add a dashboard to monitor cache hit ratios and Elasticsearch batch sizes in production to validate the performance gains and tune TTL values
Consider implementing a cache warming strategy for popular destinations during off-peak hours to ensure consistent response times during traffic spikes
